ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 650–1000L
DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS8-L1-6501000-MSSCI8-LEC-CFI-02 | MS Science: Grade 8 | Population Growth and Per-Capita Resource Consumption | Lab Experiment Challenge | Cultural Fusion & Innovation
Two Pots, One Variable
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The boy is discussing his opinion about changing one school lunch to use less water. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the boy gives to support his opinion. As you listen, you may take notes.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1:
Why does the boy bring up the food thrown out at lunch?
2QUESTION 2:
Where does the boy get the information he uses to support his idea?
3QUESTION 3:
What does the boy say about raising beef?
4QUESTION 4:
What is the boy's main reason that changing one lunch matters?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Listen for the moment the boy says why the food bothers him. Listen for where he learned his facts. Listen for what he says about the water in food.
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | NGSS: MS-ESS3-4 | MS Science: Grade 8
